strategy: The Castle Darkmoor Graveyard finale, the most famous team dungeon in the game. Normally a 4-person run: a hitter, supports feeding blades and Feints, and a healer riding out the interrupt cycles. It is a two-phase fight (24,000, then Haunted Malistaire heals to 48,000). (An expert solo clear is possible; the guide shows a Life solo route that leans on Sanctuary and Guardian Spirit to survive.)
First form boosts Life (+40%), so skip prisms and Shrike here and just stack Life blades and hit with Forest Lord. Ice, Myth, and Balance also work (+20%); avoid Death and Storm.

Don't cast universal blades or wards on yourself (he Steals them), don't Feint or place negative charms on him (he Cleanses them), and never place a Weakness (any Weakness Empowers him +3 pips). Use school-specific setup and Indemnity on Feints. He opens with a Mega Taunt, so plan the forced targeting.

Play around the Skeletal Dragon strip. It removes all charms and wards on Round 5 in phase one (Round 4 in phase two), so run Regenerate the turn before the dragon, then 3 blades and a hit the round after.
At below 1,000 HP he becomes Haunted Malistaire, heals to 48,000, and now resists Life. Switch to traps, a Life prism, and Shrike, and use King Artorius cards for shadow pips.

In phase two watch the end-of-Round-4 Shadow Steal (1,600 damage, heals him) and the dragon strip; keep your health up or carry Guardian Spirit, and hold a Treasure-Card Doom and Gloom active on the kill to cap his revive at 25,050.
cheatNotes: He Mega Taunts the first wizard, forcing your hits onto that player; keep them healed and shielded.
He doesn't stay dead; the first defeat revives him as Haunted Malistaire, so plan for a second phase.
Casting a universal blade or positive ward on yourself makes him Steal it; use school-specific Life blades or protect them.
Pet-cast blades and wards also get Stolen, so watch your pet's may-casts.
On a multi-target blade or ward, only the caster's copy is stolen; anyone else keeps theirs.
Wards that are secondary effects, like Abominable Weaver's, do not trigger the steal.
Placing a universal Trap like Feint makes him Cleanse it off; protect Feints with Indemnity.
A universal negative Charm makes him Cleanse it; avoid loose weaknesses and mantles.
Pet spells, Life Feint, and secondary charm effects also get Cleansed.
All-enemy charms and wards like Mass Feint, and Infections, do not trigger the cleanse; those are the safe ways to debuff him.
Elemental or Spirit Defuse makes him Cleanse all three dispels at once; single Dispels are more reliable.
Any Weakness on him makes him Empower for 300 Death and +3 pips; never Weaken him.
Weakness pets, roshambo spells, and secondary weakness effects (Efreet) also feed his Empower; keep all weakness sources off him.
A single-target hit earns you a -55% Black Mantle afterward; keep an accuracy buffer or Cleanse it.
His own later interrupt cycle can strip that Mantle back off you before the last round.
At the end of the third cycle Round he becomes a Black Dragon and hits a random wizard for 535 Moon, destroying auras; re-apply auras after.
At the end of the fourth Round he Deadly Minotaurs a random wizard; keep your health up.
At the end of the fifth Round he auras up his own Death damage (+20/+15/+10%) and pip chance for five Rounds; burst him before it pays off.
At the start of Round 5 his Skeletal Dragon removes all charms and wards from every wizard; land your setup and hit the round after, not before.
If you carry Empowerment or Adapt, you gain a pip or power pip when he strips you; a small consolation.
When his first form dies, or drops below 1,000, Haunted Malistaire spawns at 48,000 and full heal; save burst for this phase.
This revive happens only once, even if you didn't fully defeat the first form.
If you kill only Haunted first, he revives at 100 health with Haunted's stats and delays his shadow cycle a Round; a small opening.
A Treasure-Card Doom and Gloom present at his defeat cuts the revive to 25,050; bring one to ease phase two.
A regular Doom and Gloom won't push the revive below 48,000; the treasure version is what shrinks it.
Infections and other negative healing charms can lower his revived health further if the first form is still up.
Haunted keeps all the above cheats except the primary interrupt cycle.
His Skeletal Dragon randomly drops a +100% universal trap on Malistaire and allies; that's a free trap you can hit into.
In phase two he Shadowblades himself at the end of Round 1.
He Shadowspears himself at the end of Round 2, stacking damage.
At the end of Round 3 he drops an all-enemies Shadow Trap on you.
At the end of Round 4 he casts Shadow Steal for 1,600 and heals the full amount; keep your health up and time your kill before it lands.
At the start of Round 4 in phase two his dragon again strips all charms and wards; land your finisher before it.
